name: Arges
description: Arges, whose name signifies 'Brightness' or 'Lightning,' was one of the three Cyclopes, sons of Uranus (Heaven) and Gaia (Earth). These primordial giants were renowned for their immense strength, skill in craftsmanship, and single, prominent eye in the center of their foreheads. Arges, along with his brothers Brontes (Thunder) and Steropes (Lightning), were imprisoned by their father Uranus due to their monstrous appearance. They were later freed by Cronus, only to be imprisoned again in Tartarus. Zeus eventually freed them to aid him in his war against the Titans, the Titanomachy. In gratitude for their release and recognizing their prodigious skills, the Cyclopes forged for Zeus his thunderbolts, for Poseidon his trident, and for Hades his helmet of invisibility. Arges specifically is most often associated with the act of making things shine, adding brightness to the weapons and tools forged in the Cyclopean workshops. He represents the power and skill necessary to create weapons of immense power and divine quality. His contribution to Zeus's arsenal was crucial in securing victory for the Olympian gods.
subtitle: Cyclops, forger of Zeus's lightning.
appearance: Arges is depicted as a massive, muscular giant with a single, large eye in the center of his forehead. His skin is rough and weathered, often depicted as a dark gray or bronze color, reflecting his association with the forge. He possesses immense physical strength and typically wears simple clothing suited for working in a fiery environment, such as a leather apron and minimal garments. He carries tools of his trade, like hammers and tongs.
habitat: Arges resides within volcanic forges, deep within the earth or on volcanic islands, where he crafts weapons and tools for the gods.
behavior: Arges is a diligent and focused craftsman, dedicated to his work. While possessing immense strength, he is not typically aggressive unless provoked or disturbed during his work. He is loyal to those who treat him with respect, particularly Zeus, and diligently fulfills his duties. He is taciturn and prefers the company of his brothers, Brontes and Steropes.
weaknesses: Vulnerability to targeted strikes to his single eye

keyMyths.title: Forging Zeus's Thunderbolt
keyMyths.description: Arges, along with his brothers, forged Zeus's thunderbolt during the Titanomachy, providing the king of the gods with a weapon of unparalleled power. This act was crucial in securing the victory of the Olympians over the Titans. The thunderbolt became Zeus's signature weapon, symbolizing his authority and might.

keyMyths.title: Imprisonment and Liberation
keyMyths.description: Arges and his brothers were imprisoned by their father Uranus and later by Cronus, reflecting the fear and resentment their monstrous appearance inspired. Zeus freed them from Tartarus, recognizing their potential as powerful allies and skilled craftsmen. This liberation marked a turning point in the Titanomachy.

keyMyths.title: Crafting Divine Weapons
keyMyths.description: Besides Zeus's thunderbolt, Arges and his brothers also forged Poseidon's trident and Hades' helmet of invisibility, demonstrating their exceptional craftsmanship. These weapons further solidified the power of the Olympian gods and their control over the cosmos. Their skills were highly valued by the Olympians.
